sections.title: Section 1
sections.description: A woman makes a reservation at a restaurant.

question_groups.instructions: Complete the table below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
question_groups.title: Delano's Restaurant — Reservation Form
question_groups.form_text:
Opening hours:
• Monday to Thursday – 5:00 to 10:00 pm
• Friday to Sunday – 5:00 to midnight

Full name for reservation: ___1___

Contact phone number: ___2___

Reservation details:
• Date: Friday, February 7
• Time: 8:00 pm
• No. of people: 9 adults, plus two ___3___

Special event: ___4___

Special requirements:
• Do not serve: ___5___
• ___6___ seating needed

question_groups.instructions: Choose TWO letters A–E. Which TWO starters does the woman choose?

questions.text: Which TWO starters does the woman choose? (First choice)
Which TWO starters does the woman choose? (Second choice)
Which TWO special requests does the woman make? (First request)
Which TWO special requests does the woman make? (Second request)

sections.title: Section 2
sections.description: A manager gives a workplace induction talk to new employees.

question_groups.instructions: Answer the questions below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.

questions.text: What will the speaker talk about at the end of the presentation?
What will employees get if they don't follow the safety rules?
Where are the incident report forms stored?
Who signs the timesheet for a part-time employee?

question_groups.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.text: Which of the following does the speaker NOT talk about as bad behaviour?
Office staff need to wear a white company shirt and ...
In the workshop, staff must wear gloves …
If the fire alarm sounds, staff …

options.label: A
options.text: may not take anything with them

options.label: B
options.text: may take their keys and phone with them

options.label: C
options.text: must exit through the main glass doors

questions.text: The next fire drill will be held …
The next part of the talk will be about …

options.label: A
options.text: how to ask questions at work

options.label: B
options.text: how to report a dangerous situation

sections.title: Section 3
sections.description: Two students discuss staff responsibilities and semester schedule at their language school.

question_groups.instructions: Match the responsibility to the person. Write the correct letter, A–G, next to each person.

options.label: F
options.text: notes any changes to the syllabus

questions.text: Maureen
Paul
Jason
Kelly

question_groups.instructions: Complete the Semester Schedule below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
question_groups.title: Semester Schedule
question_groups.note_text:
Week 1 — Monday: Give students the ___25___

Week 4 — short personal ___26___

Week 6 — First ___27___ due

Week 12 — Thursday: Exam
            Friday: ___28___

Week 13 — Teachers mark assessments in the ___29___

Week 14 — Students get their ___30___

sections.title: Section 4
sections.description: A lecture about driverless (autonomous) car technology.

question_groups.instructions: Fill in the table below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
question_groups.title: Driverless Car Levels
question_groups.table_text:
Level 0 — Driver only:
Driver controls the pedals and ___31___
Already incorporate a lot of software

Level 1 — Assisted:
Help with parking, electronic ___32___

Level 2 — Partial Automation:
Can stay in one lane and at a steady distance from the car in front
Need careful driver monitoring

Level 3 — ___33___ Automation:
Will usually not need driver input; car may sometimes request help from the driver

Level 4 — High Automation:
Will only be able to operate in ___34___

Level 5 — Full Automation:
No driver needed, no pedals or steering wheel

question_groups.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.text: Which option is NOT stated to be a problem for existing robot car models?

options.label: B
options.text: Dealing with unknown changes en route

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT true?

options.label: A
options.text: In 2012, US and UK people liked autonomous cars more than Germans did

options.label: B
options.text: In 2015, more Germans liked than didn't like autonomous cars

options.label: C
options.text: In 2015, about a third of the people surveyed liked autonomous cars

questions.text: What percentage of car crashes happen because of driver error?
What will the main problem be for driverless cars in the transition period?

options.label: C
options.text: Deciding what to do in a crash situation

question_groups.instructions: Choose TWO letters, A–E. Which of the following are advantages of driverless cars?

questions.text: Which of the following are advantages of driverless cars? (First advantage)
Which of the following are advantages of driverless cars? (Second advantage)
